pkg/framework.purpose: Shared low-level infrastructure layer providing the generic HTTP transport pipeline (httptransport), Ent transaction management (entutils), distributed locks (lockr), RFC 7807 error encoding (commonhttp), OTel instrumentation helpers (clickhouseotel, tracex), and pgx pool wiring (pgdriver). All domain packages under openmeter/ depend on this layer; it must not import them.

patterns.name: httptransport decode/operate/encode pipeline
patterns.description: Every HTTP endpoint uses httptransport.NewHandler(operation, decoder, responseEncoder, errorEncoder, opts...) — never implement ServeHTTP directly in a handler struct.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo for all Ent adapter DB access
patterns.description: Every adapter method wraps its body in entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, client, func(tx *entdb.Tx) (*T, error){...}) so it rebinds to any transaction already carried in ctx.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a.client, func(tx *entdb.Tx) (*Entity, error) { return tx.Entity.Create()...; })

patterns.name: operation.Operation function type with Middleware
patterns.description: Business logic is expressed as operation.Operation[Req, Resp] (a plain func) and composed via operation.Chain/Middleware. Never put business logic in middleware.
patterns.example: var op operation.Operation[Request, Response] = func(ctx context.Context, req Request) (Response, error) { ... }

patterns.name: GenericErrorEncoder chain
patterns.description: Error encoders return bool; false passes to the next encoder. Add domain errors as models.Generic* sentinel types mapped in commonhttp.GenericErrorEncoder; never write status codes directly inside handler code.
patterns.example: func EncodeError(ctx context.Context, err error, w http.ResponseWriter) bool { return commonhttp.HandleErrorIfTypeMatches[*MyError](ctx, http.StatusBadRequest, err, w) }

patterns.name: Locker requires active Postgres transaction in ctx
patterns.description: lockr.Locker.LockForTX(ctx, key) calls pg_advisory_xact_lock inside the Ent tx already in ctx. Always call inside entutils.TransactingRepo; never call from outside a transaction.
patterns.example: entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, client, func(tx *entdb.Tx) (*T, error) { if err := locker.LockForTX(ctx, key); err != nil { return nil, err }; ... })

patterns.name: tracex.Start + Wrap for span lifecycle
patterns.description: Use tracex.Start/Wrap instead of tracer.Start directly; Wrap records errors, sets span status, and handles panics automatically.
patterns.example: return tracex.Start(ctx, tracer, "svc.Op", func(s *tracex.Span[*Entity]) (*Entity, error) { return s.Wrap(doWork(s.Ctx())) })

patterns.name: Functional options (Option interface) for configuration
patterns.description: Packages like pgdriver and clickhouseotel expose Option interfaces for configuration; call Validate() before first use to surface nil Conn/Meter/Tracer early.

key_file_guides.role: TransactingRepo/TxDriver implementation; ctx-propagated transaction reuse with savepoints for nesting
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never call creator.Tx() directly; always use TransactingRepo. Never store *entdb.Tx on adapter struct.

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin definitions (IDMixin ULID char(26), NamespaceMixin, TimeMixin, MetadataMixin, AnnotationsMixin)
key_file_guides.watch_for: All entities must compose these mixins; never add id/namespace/created_at fields manually on schema.

key_file_guides.role: RFC 7807 problem-detail error chain: GenericErrorEncoder, HandleIssueIfHTTPStatusKnown, ValidationIssue HTTP status attribute
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always use WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute on ValidationIssue; without it HandleIssueIfHTTPStatusKnown returns false and falls through.

key_file_guides.role: Transaction-scoped advisory lock via pg_advisory_xact_lock; key hashed with xxhash
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must be called inside an active Ent transaction; calling outside returns error. Don't use context.WithTimeout — use pgdriver.WithLockTimeout instead.

key_file_guides.role: Connection-scoped advisory lock (pg_advisory_lock/unlock) needing a dedicated *sql.Conn
key_file_guides.watch_for: Not goroutine-safe under high contention; always call Close() to release the dedicated connection.

key_file_guides.role: Generic Handler[Request,Response] implementing http.Handler; wraps operation with decode/encode
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never implement ServeHTTP directly in domain httpdriver — always construct via httptransport.NewHandler.

key_file_guides.role: Constructs project-wide *sql.DB from pgxpool with OTel tracing, metrics, and optional lock_timeout
key_file_guides.watch_for: Don't set MaxIdleConns > 0 on the returned *sql.DB — pgx pool manages idle connections; dual management causes leaks.

key_file_guides.role: Generic OTel span wrapper with error recording, status, and panic recovery
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always pass s.Ctx() through inside Wrap callbacks; never introduce context.Background() there.

anti_patterns: Importing openmeter/* domain packages from any pkg/framework sub-package — creates import cycles; this layer must be a leaf dependency
Using context.Background() or context.TODO() inside adapter callbacks or Wrap functions — always propagate the caller ctx
Calling creator.Tx() or storing *entdb.Tx on adapter structs instead of using TransactingRepo — falls off the ctx-bound transaction
Adding ORDER BY clauses on Ent queries before calling .Cursor() — entcursor appends its own order producing undefined combined ordering
Implementing business logic inside operation.Middleware — middleware is for cross-cutting concerns (auth, logging, tracing) only

decisions.decision: TransactingRepo reads transaction from ctx rather than accepting *entdb.Tx as a parameter
decisions.rationale: Ent transactions propagate implicitly via ctx; explicit *entdb.Tx parameters leak tx plumbing into every call site and cannot enforce nesting via savepoints.
Enforces consistent request validation, error encoding, and OTel tracing across all ~60+ endpoints without duplicating boilerplate in each handler.

decisions.decision: Two advisory lock types (Locker for tx-scoped, SessionLocker for connection-scoped)
decisions.rationale: Different lifetime requirements: billing operations need lock released on tx commit/rollback (Locker), while some admin flows need lock outlive individual transactions (SessionLocker).

code_examples.scenario: New HTTP endpoint: full decode/operate/encode handler construction
